title: Ch 7.5 — Collections & toString: Dungeon Crawler
description:
Combine everything from Chapter 7: private fields, constructors,
getters/setters with validation, toString(), and ArrayList.

You'll build a Monster class and a Dungeon class that manages
a collection of monsters.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Class: Monster (package-private)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
  Private fields:
    • String name
    • int health

  Constructor: Monster(String name, int health)
    — Use this. Clamp health >= 0.

  Getters:
    • String getName()
    • int getHealth()

  Method:
    • void takeDamage(int amount)
        — If amount < 0, ignore it (do nothing).
        — Otherwise subtract from health; clamp at 0.

    • boolean isDefeated()
        — returns true if health == 0

  Override toString():
    • returns "<n> [HP=<health>]"
    • example: "Goblin [HP=30]"

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Class: Dungeon (public)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
  Private fields:
    • String name
    • java.util.ArrayList<Monster> monsters

  Constructor: Dungeon(String name)
    — Use this. Initialize monsters to an empty ArrayList.

  Methods:
    • void addMonster(Monster m)
        — Adds the monster to the list.

    • int getMonsterCount()
        — Returns how many monsters are in the list.

    • String listMonsters()
        — Builds and returns a multi-line String listing all
          monsters, one per line, using each monster's toString().
        — If the dungeon is empty, returns "The dungeon is empty."
        — Example (2 monsters):
            "Goblin [HP=30]\nDragon [HP=200]"

    • int attackAll(int damage)
        — Calls takeDamage(damage) on every monster in the list.
        — After attacking, REMOVES all defeated monsters from the list.
        — Returns the number of monsters that were defeated (removed).

  Override toString():
    • returns "<n> (X monsters)"
    • example: "Dark Cave (3 monsters)"

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• You need: import java.util.ArrayList; at the top of your file.
• Make Monster package-private (no public keyword). Dungeon is public.
• For attackAll(), be careful removing from a list while iterating.
  TIP: use an Iterator, or loop backwards, or build a list of
  defeated monsters first and then call removeAll().
• listMonsters() separates lines with "\n" (no trailing newline).
